problem_statement: MAF receipt parity claims need a typed static witness that maps Python receipt schemas to Rust crate surfaces before any Python-to-Rust runtime binding, subprocess, CLI, or execution authority can be reconsidered.
success_criteria: MafReceiptParityWitness validates against a public JSON schema and Foundation Mode example

MafReceiptParityWitness binds Python schema refs, Rust crate manifest refs, Rust crate entry refs, canonical source digests, parity mappings, gap refs, UAO refs, LifeMeaningJudgment refs, and receipt refs

MafReceiptParityWitness blocks PyO3 binding, subprocess execution, CLI execution, Rust crate execution, Python Rust imports, external connector calls, network calls, secret access, filesystem writes, runtime dispatch, canonical state mutation, terminal closure, and success claims

MafReceiptParityWitness rejects authority drift, schema digest drift, crate digest drift, missing future witnesses, gap closure without evidence, secret markers, receipt ref drift, and summary count drift
MafReceiptParityWitness is covered by a focused validator, root test, protocol manifest entry, proof coverage matrix entry, and CI hook
constraints: runtime_binding_claimed, rust_execution_performed, and python_to_rust_call_path_claimed remain false
required future witnesses include ABI/CLI contract, subprocess effect boundary, deterministic fixture parity, and failure receipt path
each Python schema ref and Rust crate surface digest must match repository-local source files
all parity mappings must retain open gap refs and runtime_binding_allowed false
